query: Latest in operations management, resource optimization, and procurement
raw_intelligence:
**AI integration as an operational layer** dominates latest trends in operations management, resource optimization, and procurement, shifting from standalone tools to embedded workflows for real-time decision-making and efficiency.[1][2][6]

### Operations Management
Control towers are evolving into action centers that recommend corrective actions, trigger automated workflows, and integrate with WMS, TMS, and procurement systems, moving beyond visibility to proactive orchestration.[1][4] Manufacturing operations management (MOM) software market is projected to grow from $18.13 billion in 2025 to $35.55 billion by 2035, driven by unified platforms for production planning, quality management, and analytics amid supply chain volatility.[2] Agentic AI workflows coordinate specialized AI agents for end-to-end automation, reducing manual overhead in repeatable operations and addressing long ROI cycles.[6][9] Asset maintenance trends emphasize predictive maintenance with 68% of organizations increasing budgets, offline-capable apps for frontline workers, and AI-driven condition-based workflows to cut unplanned downtime costs.[3]

### Resource Optimization
AI embeds into TMS for routing alternatives, WMS for task sequencing based on congestion and labor, and AMR fleets via centralized orchestration prioritizing uptime and integration over hardware innovation.[1] Cloud-based MOM software enables scalability, remote monitoring, and predictive maintenance via advanced analytics, optimizing resource utilization and minimizing waste in smart factories.[2] Supply chain autonomy rises with AI handling real-time monitoring, route optimization, and inventory replenishment, freeing managers for strategic roles like relationship-building and prevention.[4] Digitization yields 20% labor efficiency gains, with voice-enabled AI capturing retiring workers' expertise.[3]

### Procurement
AI identifies supplier risk signals early and accelerates tasks from weeks to days, evolving procurement roles amid tariffs reshaping global trade priorities and delaying some digital shifts.[1][4][5] Chief Procurement Officers use AI for real-time Scope 3 emissions monitoring and supplier compliance, making ESG operational.[6] Risk modeling integrates into routine planning for weather, cyber, carrier, and energy disruptions faced in 2025.[1]

| Area | Key 2026 Driver | Impact |
|------|-----------------|--------|
| Operations | Agentic AI & control towers | 20%+ efficiency gains, reduced downtime[1][3] |
| Resources | Orchestrated automation | Stable throughput, labor role shifts[1][2] |
| Procurement | AI risk detection | Faster decisions, ESG compliance[1][4][6] |

Tariffs test supply chain resilience, prioritizing tactical moves over full digital transformation in some cases.[5] Successful firms modernize data layers and focus on execution for resilience.[1]
